_comment: A fictional profile, used for the screenshots in the docs and as something to point the tool at before you have written your own. Nobody's real resume lives in this repo.

basics.name: Jane Smith
basics.label: Senior Software Engineer | Go · Python · Distributed Systems
basics.summary: Senior backend engineer with 9 years building and operating distributed systems in Go and Python. Works across the stack — API design, data modelling, and the observability that keeps a service honest at 3am — on teams of 5 to 20. Most recently led the billing platform rewrite at a payments company, moving 40 million monthly transactions onto a new ledger with no customer-visible downtime.

work.name: Northwind Payments
highlights: Led the billing platform rewrite — migrated 40M monthly transactions from a legacy ledger to an event-sourced service in Go, with zero customer-visible downtime across a 6-month cutover.
Cut p99 checkout latency from 840ms to 190ms by replacing synchronous fan-out with a Kafka consumer group and a read model in Redis.
Designed the double-entry ledger schema now backing 4 product lines, including the reconciliation job that closes the books nightly against 3 payment processors.
Grew the integration suite to 1,400 tests against real Postgres and Kafka, taking the median CI run from 22 minutes to 6.
Ran the on-call rotation for 12 engineers — wrote the incident review practice, and reduced repeat pages by 60% over three quarters.
Mentored 4 engineers through their first production services; two have since become tech leads.

work.name: Contoso Logistics
highlights: Built the route-optimisation API in Python and Go, serving 9,000 daily dispatch requests across 120 depots.
Replaced 14 cron jobs with a single scheduler service, eliminating the overnight failure class that caused most Monday incidents.
Introduced distributed tracing across 11 services, which located a connection-pool leak that had caused intermittent timeouts for over a year.
Wrote the ETL pipeline moving 2M shipment events nightly into ClickHouse, cutting the analytics team's query times from minutes to seconds.

work.name: Fabrikam Software
highlights: Shipped the customer-facing reporting UI in TypeScript and React, used weekly by 3,000 accounts.
Migrated the primary datastore from MySQL to PostgreSQL over 5 months, with a dual-write phase and no data loss.
Automated the release pipeline, taking deploys from a fortnightly manual ritual to 8 per week.
